cards.q: What are the levels of CLAUDE.md hierarchy?
cards.a: Managed policy (OS app-support dir, can't be excluded) → Project (./CLAUDE.md or ./.claude/CLAUDE.md, shared via VCS) → User (~/.claude/CLAUDE.md, personal) → Local (./CLAUDE.local.md, gitignored). Files concatenate, they do not override. Delivered as a user message after the system prompt.

cards.q: Where should a team-wide /review slash command be stored in 2026?
cards.a: Preferred: .claude/skills/review/SKILL.md (skills now subsume custom commands). Legacy: .claude/commands/review.md still works. Both go in the project repo (version-controlled). NOT ~/.claude/ which is user-only.

cards.q: What does the context: fork frontmatter option do in a skill?
cards.a: Runs the skill in an isolated subagent (built-in agents: Explore, Plan, general-purpose), preventing verbose output (codebase analysis, brainstorming) from polluting the main conversation context.

Modular rule files with optional YAML 'paths:' frontmatter (e.g., paths: ['**/*.test.tsx']) for path-scoped loading. Better than directory-level CLAUDE.md when conventions span multiple directories. Symlinks supported. ~/.claude/rules/ for user-level.

cards.q: When should you use plan mode vs direct execution?
cards.a: Plan mode: large-scale changes, multi-file mods, architectural decisions, multiple valid approaches. Direct execution: single-file fixes, well-understood changes with clear scope. Activate via --permission-mode plan or Shift+Tab. ExitPlanMode tool returns the plan; user must accept.

cards.q: How do you run Claude Code non-interactively in CI?
cards.a: Use -p (or --print) to process the prompt and exit. For 2026: --bare is recommended (skips hooks/skills/plugins/MCP/auto-memory/CLAUDE.md autoload). Add --output-format json (or stream-json with --include-partial-messages) and --json-schema '<schema>' for structured output.

cards.q: What is the strictest CI permission mode?
cards.a: dontAsk, denies anything not explicitly listed in permissions.allow. acceptEdits auto-approves edits + filesystem commands (mkdir/touch/rm/mv/cp/sed). bypassPermissions allows everything (dangerous; rejected for root in DevContainer).

cards.q: Why should a code reviewer be a separate Claude session from the generator?
cards.a: Self-review limitations: a model retains reasoning context from generation, making it less likely to question its own decisions. An independent instance catches more issues. For multi-pass review, split per-file local pass + cross-file integration pass.

cards.q: What does the allowed-tools frontmatter do in a SKILL.md?
cards.a: Pre-approves specific tools (with permission rule syntax like Bash(git add *)) while the skill is active. Restricts skill execution scope. To DENY tools, use permissions.deny in settings.json, not skill frontmatter.

cards.q: What is @import used for in CLAUDE.md?
cards.a: @path/to/file references external standards files to keep CLAUDE.md modular. Recursive max depth 5. Approval dialog on first encounter. Pattern: import @AGENTS.md inside CLAUDE.md (Claude reads only CLAUDE.md, but follows imports).

cards.q: How do you diagnose inconsistent behavior across team members?
cards.a: Check if instructions are in user-level (~/.claude/CLAUDE.md or ~/.claude/skills/) instead of project-level. User-level config is not shared. Use /memory to verify which memory files are loaded. /init can scaffold a shareable .claude/CLAUDE.md.

cards.q: What's the structure of a Claude Code plugin?
cards.a: Manifest at <plugin>/.claude-plugin/plugin.json (name, description, version, author). Components at plugin ROOT (not inside .claude-plugin/): skills/, commands/, agents/, hooks/hooks.json, .mcp.json, output-styles/, bin/. Use ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} for paths. Plugin skills are auto-namespaced as 'plugin-name:skill-name'.

cards.q: How do you ship a destructive /deploy skill that runs in isolation with only Bash + Read?
cards.a: Create .claude/skills/deploy/SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter: context: fork (isolated subagent), allowed-tools: ['Bash', 'Read']. Commands don't support tool restrictions. ~/.claude/skills/ would be user-only (not shareable). CLAUDE.md can't restrict tools per workflow.

cards.q: Where does Claude Code's auto memory live?
cards.a: ~/.claude/projects/<encoded-cwd>/memory/MEMORY.md plus topic files. The first 200 lines OR 25 KB load each session. Requires v2.1.59+. Toggle with autoMemoryEnabled setting or CLAUDE_CODE_DISABLE_AUTO_MEMORY=1.

cards.q: What's the difference between a Skill and a Sub-agent?
cards.a: Skill: instructions injected into the CURRENT context window when triggered. Sub-agent: a separate Claude instance with its OWN context window, system prompt, and tools. Bridge: 'context: fork' on a skill spawns a subagent for that skill's execution.

cards.q: How do plugin skills handle name collisions with project skills?
cards.a: They don't collide, plugin skills are ALWAYS namespaced as 'plugin-name:skill-name'. Project (.claude/skills/) and user (~/.claude/skills/) skills are unnamespaced; on collision precedence is Enterprise > Personal > Project.

cards.q: What does --bare do, and when do you use it?
cards.a: Skips hooks, skills, plugins, MCP servers, auto memory, and CLAUDE.md autoload, only flags apply. Skips OAuth/keychain too (auth must come from ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or apiKeyHelper in --settings). Recommended for CI; will become the default behavior of -p in a future release.

cards.q: How do you get JSON-schema-validated output from claude -p?
cards.a: claude -p '...' --output-format json --json-schema '<schema>'. The validated output appears in the structured_output field of the JSON result. The result also includes total_cost_usd and session_id.

crash.val: Managed (OS app-support, can't exclude) → Project (./CLAUDE.md or ./.claude/CLAUDE.md) → User (~/.claude/CLAUDE.md) → Local (./CLAUDE.local.md). FILES CONCATENATE.
Project = shared via VCS. User-level (~/.claude/) is PERSONAL ONLY: not shared with teammates.
@path/file recursive max 5. Approval dialog on first encounter. claudeMdExcludes can skip ancestor files (managed cannot be excluded).
~/.claude/projects/<repo>/memory/MEMORY.md + topic files. First 200 lines / 25 KB load each session. v2.1.59+.
.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md creates /name. Custom commands merged into Skills. Old .claude/commands/*.md still works.
name, description, when_to_use, argument-hint, allowed-tools, model, effort, context: fork, agent, hooks, paths, disable-model-invocation, user-invocable
$ARGUMENTS, $N, ${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}, ${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}. Dynamic: !`bash` for shell preprocess.
Enterprise > Personal (~/.claude/skills/) > Project (.claude/skills/). Plugin skills auto-namespaced as plugin:skill.
Run skill in isolated subagent (built-ins: Explore, Plan, general-purpose). Prevents verbose output from polluting main context.
.claude/agents/<name>.md. Frontmatter: tools, disallowedTools, model, permissionMode, mcpServers, hooks, maxTurns, skills, memory, effort, background, isolation, color.
Manifest <plugin>/.claude-plugin/plugin.json. Components at plugin ROOT: skills/, agents/, hooks/hooks.json, .mcp.json, bin/. Use ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}.
Modular rule files with optional YAML paths: glob frontmatter (e.g. **/*.test.tsx). Loads only when matching files touched.
Plan mode: large multi-file architectural decisions, multiple valid approaches. Direct execution: single-file, well-scoped changes.
-p / --print for headless. --bare skips local config (recommended CI). --output-format json + --json-schema → structured_output field.
default, acceptEdits (edits + mkdir/touch/rm/mv/cp/sed), plan, auto, dontAsk (strictest CI), bypassPermissions.
Check if instructions are in ~/.claude/ (user-only) instead of project-level. Use /memory to verify loaded files.
Skill = instructions injected into CURRENT context. Sub-agent = separate Claude with its OWN context. Bridge: context: fork on a skill spawns a subagent.
allowed-tools: ['Bash(git add *)', 'Read'] in frontmatter pre-approves while skill active. To DENY use permissions.deny in settings.
Independent reviewer instance catches more than the generator reviewing itself. Same session retains reasoning context.

On re-runs after new commits, pass prior review findings into context and instruct Claude to report only new or still-unaddressed issues. Same pattern for test generation: pass existing test files in context to avoid duplicate scenarios.
User-level config is NOT shared. Put team-shared content in .claude/ at project root.
Test files spread across dirs need .claude/rules/ with globs, not directory-bound CLAUDE.md.

examQs.q: Your codebase has React components (functional style), API handlers (async/await), and test files spread throughout. You want Claude to apply correct conventions automatically per file type. What's the best approach?
opts: Create .claude/rules/ files with YAML 'paths:' frontmatter glob patterns for each file type
Put all conventions in the root CLAUDE.md under separate headers, relying on Claude to infer which section applies
Create skills in .claude/skills/ for each code type with conventions in SKILL.md files
Place a separate CLAUDE.md in each subdirectory with that area's conventions
examQs.explain: Path-specific rules with glob patterns (paths: ['**/*.test.tsx']) automatically apply conventions based on file paths regardless of directory location, essential for files spread throughout a codebase. Inferring sections is unreliable, skills need invocation, and directory-level CLAUDE.md can't handle files spread across many directories.

examQs.q: Your CI pipeline runs 'claude "Analyze this pull request"' but hangs indefinitely. You want the strictest, most CI-friendly mode that ignores local dev config. What's the right invocation?
opts: claude -p "Analyze..." --bare --output-format json --permission-mode dontAsk --max-turns 5 --allowedTools "Read,Bash"
Set the environment variable CLAUDE_HEADLESS=true before running the command
Redirect stdin from /dev/null: claude "Analyze..." < /dev/null
Add the --batch flag: claude --batch "Analyze..."
examQs.explain: -p / --print is the documented headless mode. --bare (2026) skips hooks/skills/plugins/MCP/auto-memory/CLAUDE.md autoload, recommended for CI. --permission-mode dontAsk is the strictest mode (denies anything not in permissions.allow). --output-format json gives structured CI output. The other options reference non-existent features.

examQs.q: You want a /deploy skill that performs a destructive production deployment. It should run in isolation and only have access to Bash and Read tools. Where and how do you configure this?
opts: Create .claude/skills/deploy/SKILL.md with context: fork and allowed-tools: ['Bash', 'Read'] in the YAML frontmatter
Create .claude/commands/deploy.md and add tool restrictions in the root CLAUDE.md
Create the skill in ~/.claude/skills/deploy/ so each developer can customize their deployment flow
Add the deployment instructions directly to the root CLAUDE.md with a section header 'Deployment'
examQs.explain: Skills in .claude/skills/ with SKILL.md support context: fork (isolated execution in a subagent) and allowed-tools (restricting available tools). Commands don't support tool restrictions per workflow. User-level config isn't shared. CLAUDE.md can't restrict tools per workflow.

examQs.q: Your CI review re-runs on each new commit but keeps reporting issues that were already addressed. How do you prevent duplicate comments?
opts: Include prior review findings in context and instruct Claude to report only new or still-unaddressed issues
Cache all previous review comments and filter duplicates in a post-processing step
Run reviews only on the diff between the latest commit and the previous review
Add a deduplication step that compares new findings against a database of past findings
examQs.explain: Including prior findings in context and instructing Claude to report only new/unaddressed issues is the recommended approach, leverages model understanding rather than building external infrastructure. Post-processing and databases add unnecessary complexity.

examQs.q: You've built a deploy automation that includes a /deploy skill, a deploy-sentinel sub-agent, a PreToolUse hook for safety, and a custom MCP server for your deploy platform. Multiple teams across the org need it. What's the right packaging?
opts: Bundle them as a Claude Code plugin: <plugin>/.claude-plugin/plugin.json plus skills/, agents/, hooks/hooks.json, and .mcp.json at plugin root; distribute via marketplace or git
Put each component in ~/.claude/ on every team member's machine and document setup steps
Create a shared .claude/ directory in a separate repo and have each team symlink to it
Build everything as a single SKILL.md and rely on context: fork to spawn the subagent inline
examQs.explain: Plugins are the 2026 packaging unit for shipping multiple component types together. Manifest at <plugin>/.claude-plugin/plugin.json; components at plugin root; ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT} for paths. Plugin skills are auto-namespaced as 'plugin-name:skill-name'. Distribute via marketplace, git URL, --plugin-dir, or --plugin-url (zip). The other options either fragment distribution or can't bundle hooks/MCP.
